# Test-Quality Review: `lib/features/ai/state/` + `test/features/ai/state/`

**Scope:** All implementation files under `lib/features/ai/state/` and their counterpart test files under `test/features/ai/state/`.
**Generated by automated test-quality review (static analysis)**

## File size / split opportunities

- [x] **[HIGH]** `test/features/ai/state/unified_ai_controller_test.dart` ‚Äî 2470 lines for a single test file far exceeds the ~500-line preferred size. The file covers four logically separate provider families that can be mirror-split to match the logical seams in the implementation:
  - `unified_ai_controller_test.dart` ‚Äî `UnifiedAiState` equality + `UnifiedAiController` (~lines 167‚Äì597)
  - `available_skills_for_entity_provider_test.dart` ‚Äî `availableSkillsForEntityProvider` + `hasAvailableSkillsProvider` (~lines 599‚Äì1511)
  - `trigger_skill_provider_test.dart` ‚Äî `triggerSkillProvider` (~lines 1513‚Äì2469)
  - `trigger_new_inference_provider_test.dart` ‚Äî `triggerNewInferenceProvider` (~lines 686‚Äì947)
  The shared `_TestBench` / helper setup should be extracted into a file-local helper to stay DRY across the split files.
  **RESOLVED (adapted):** the impl was split first so the test split honors the one-test-file-per-source rule ‚Äî the four skill/trigger provider families moved to `lib/features/ai/state/skill_trigger_providers.dart` (304 lines; `unified_ai_controller.dart` now 343), and their test groups (hasAvailableSkills, availableSkillsForEntity, triggerSkill, triggerNewInference, linked-entity inference) moved to the new mirror `skill_trigger_providers_test.dart` (2 654 lines); `unified_ai_controller_test.dart` keeps the state-equality and controller groups (633 lines).

- [x] **[HIGH]** `test/features/ai/state/settings/inference_model_form_controller_dirty_state_test.dart` (308 lines) and `test/features/ai/state/settings/inference_provider_form_controller_dirty_state_test.dart` (323 lines) both violate the one-test-file-per-source-file rule. Their tests must be merged back into their respective primary test files (`inference_model_form_controller_test.dart` and `inference_provider_form_controller_test.dart`).

- [x] **[MED]** `lib/features/ai/state/unified_ai_controller.dart` ‚Äî 635 lines. Three conceptually separate provider families live here: `UnifiedAiController` + `triggerNewInferenceProvider` (controller wiring), `availableSkillsForEntityProvider` + `hasAvailableSkillsProvider` (entity capability queries), and `triggerSkillProvider` (skill dispatch). Splitting into `unified_ai_controller.dart`, `available_skills_providers.dart`, and `trigger_skill_provider.dart` would bring each file under ~250 lines. **RESOLVED:** (stale) the split happened ‚Äî `unified_ai_controller.dart` is 345 lines of controller wiring, and the skills/trigger providers live in `skill_trigger_providers.dart` (300 lines) with their own mirror.

- [x] **[MED]** `test/features/ai/state/embedding_backfill_controller_test.dart` ‚Äî 1268 lines. The `backfillAgentReports` group (lines ~780‚Äì1165) is independently testable and could be extracted to `embedding_backfill_agent_reports_test.dart`, but only after resolving the one-file-per-source rule: given this rule the preferred approach would be a nested group rather than a new file. However the current file at 1268 lines is large enough to warrant investigating a logical reorganization within the single file using tighter grouping. **RESOLVED:** (assessed, no change) the file already carries nine tightly-scoped groups including a dedicated `backfillAgentReports` group ‚Äî exactly the within-file organization the item asks for, with the one-file-per-source rule blocking the alternative split.

- [x] **[LOW]** `test/features/ai/state/linked_entity_inference_test.dart` ‚Äî 710 lines covering behavior already partially exercised in `unified_ai_controller_test.dart`. The overlap (linked-entity status propagation) should be audited for redundancy; unique scenarios should be folded into the unified controller test after the split described above.
  **RESOLVED:** (stale) the audit already happened in the HIGH pass ‚Äî `linked_entity_inference_test.dart` no longer exists and its linked-entity status-propagation scenarios were folded into the `triggerSkillProvider` group of the new mirror `skill_trigger_providers_test.dart` (running/idle/error propagation for both main and linked entities at lines ~338‚Äì486). No standalone redundant file remains to audit.

## Test quality improvements

- [x] **[HIGH]** `test/features/ai/state/unified_ai_controller_test.dart` ‚Äî `MockUnifiedAiInferenceRepository` is declared inline at line 37‚Äì38. The same class is also declared inline in `test/features/ai/state/linked_entity_inference_test.dart` (line 16‚Äì17). Neither file uses the centralized `test/mocks/mocks.dart`. `MockUnifiedAiInferenceRepository` should be added to `test/mocks/mocks.dart` and the inline declarations removed from both files.

- [x] **[HIGH]** `test/features/ai/state/unified_ai_controller_test.dart` ‚Äî 18 manual `getIt.isRegistered` / `getIt.unregister` / `getIt.registerSingleton` calls in `setUp` (lines 96‚Äì124). This is precisely the anti-pattern that `setUpTestGetIt()` / `tearDownTestGetIt()` from `test/widget_test_utils.dart` is designed to replace.

- [x] **[HIGH]** `test/features/ai/state/unified_ai_controller_test.dart` ‚Äî `AiConfigPrompt` is constructed verbatim ~8 times across tests (lines 211, 302, 427, 509, 688, 754, 853, 862 and more). Each construction is nearly identical (same `systemMessage`, `userMessage`, `defaultModelId`, `modelIds`, `createdAt`, `useReasoning`, `requiredInputData`) with only `id` and `aiResponseType` varying. A shared helper `_makePromptConfig({required String id, required AiResponseType responseType})` would eliminate the copy-paste.

- [x] **[HIGH]** `test/features/ai/state/profile_automation_providers_test.dart` ‚Äî Lines 42‚Äì63: three tests assert only `isA<ProfileResolver>()`, `isA<ProfileAutomationResolver>()`, and `isA<ProfileAutomationService>()`. These are pure constructor smoke tests ‚Äî they prove the provider wires up without crashing but assert no behavior. Per AGENTS.md rule: "No constructor smoke tests." The tests should be replaced with or supplemented by tests that verify the resolver/service actually produces correct results (e.g., that `profileResolverProvider` returns a resolver that correctly looks up a profile by ID from a stubbed repository).

- [x] **[MED]** `test/features/ai/state/embedding_backfill_controller_test.dart` ‚Äî Line 697: `await Future<void>.delayed(Duration.zero)` inside a test that checks `state().isRunning`. This is a real-time delay in a non-I/O test and violates the fake-time policy. The test relies on observing that `isRunning` is set to `true` between the first and second call; this should be driven by `async.flushMicrotasks()` inside `fakeAsync`, or by restructuring the test to observe the state transition deterministically rather than relying on a scheduler yield. **RESOLVED:** (stale) the test now drains via `pumpEventQueue()` with the fake-time policy noted inline.

- [x] **[MED]** `test/features/ai/state/linked_entity_inference_test.dart` ‚Äî Lines 35‚Äì57: `setUp` manually registers `DomainLogger` via `getIt.isRegistered` / `getIt.unregister` / `getIt.registerSingleton`. Same pattern as `unified_ai_controller_test.dart`. Should use `setUpTestGetIt()`. **RESOLVED:** (stale) `linked_entity_inference_test.dart` no longer exists ‚Äî its coverage was merged into the provider mirrors during the HIGH pass.

- [x] **[MED]** `test/features/ai/state/unified_ai_controller_test.dart` ‚Äî Lines 300‚Äì422: the test `'successfully runs inference and updates state'` wraps an already-async scenario inside `fakeAsync` and then drives `async.elapse(Duration(milliseconds: 10))` twice to match two `Future<void>.delayed(const Duration(milliseconds: 10))` calls embedded in the mock answer (lines 354, 356). The `Duration(milliseconds: 10)` delays inside the mock answer are real async delays placed inside `fakeAsync` ‚Äî this is the pattern described in test/README.md under "Replacing Duration.zero Yields". They should be replaced with synchronous callbacks, or the mock answer should complete immediately so the `elapse` calls are unnecessary. **RESOLVED:** (stale) the mock answer now fires its callbacks synchronously and the test drives a single `flushMicrotasks()` ‚Äî no embedded delays or `elapse` calls remain.

- [x] **[MED]** `test/features/ai/state/consts_test.dart` ‚Äî Lines 79‚Äì104: `icon` test and lines 45‚Äì77 `localizedName` test both iterate all 7 enum values with separate `expect` lines, giving 7 copy-paste expect calls per test. These can be parameterized using a `final cases = <AiResponseType, IconData>{...}` map iterated in a loop, reducing each test body to ~5 lines. **RESOLVED:** done ‚Äî one `expectedByType` table drives the icon test and the context-based `localizedName` loop, with a completeness guard that the table covers every enum value (the redundant direct-l10n string test was folded away).

- [x] **[LOW]** `test/features/ai/state/unified_ai_controller_test.dart` ‚Äî `FakeEntryControllerNull` (lines 41‚Äì43) is defined inline. If this pattern is needed in other test files it should be added to central fakes; if unique to this file it should remain but be documented.
  **RESOLVED:** (stale) the HIGH-pass split already removed `FakeEntryControllerNull` from this file ‚Äî no such class is defined here anymore. The only remnant was a dangling `/// Entry controller that returns null (entity not found).` doc comment with no class beneath it, which has now been deleted.

- [x] **[LOW]** `test/features/ai/state/linked_entity_inference_test.dart` ‚Äî Line 94: `tearDown` only unregisters `DomainLogger` but `setUp` also registers it. If the test suite is expanded the teardown may become incomplete. Should mirror the full registration/unregistration via `setUpTestGetIt()`.
  **RESOLVED:** (stale) `linked_entity_inference_test.dart` no longer exists; its coverage moved to `skill_trigger_providers_test.dart`, which already uses `setUpTestGetIt()` / `tearDownTestGetIt()` (lines ~119 and ~173), so registration and teardown are symmetric.

## Generative (Glados) testing opportunities

- [x] **[MED]** `lib/features/ai/state/unified_ai_controller.dart` ‚Äî `availableSkillsForEntityProvider` contains a pure filter function with discrete input dimensions: `hasTaskContext: bool`, `entity type: {JournalEntry, JournalAudio, Task, JournalImage, Other}`, `skill.contextPolicy: ContextPolicy enum`, `skill.requiredInputModalities: List<Modality>`, and `skill.skillType: SkillType`. The combinatorial space (5 entity types √ó 4 context policies √ó 5 modalities √ó 5 skill types) is large enough that the existing hand-written example tests (lines 949‚Äì1511) still leave gaps (e.g., a skill requiring both audio AND text modalities, or a skill with `ContextPolicy.dictionaryOnly`). A Glados property: "for any generated skill/entity pair, `availableSkillsForEntityProvider` returns the skill iff all modality, contextPolicy, and supportedType predicates are simultaneously satisfied" would be a genuine improvement. The logic is pure (given mocked entry state) and not UI or I/O bound. Tag: `glados`. **RESOLVED:** done ‚Äî a seeded `_SkillFilterScenario` (entity kind √ó linkedFrom √ó skillType √ó contextPolicy √ó modality subset) runs through the real provider per iteration and is checked against the inlined oracle predicate, covering multi-modality and fullTask-without-context combinations.

- [x] **[MED]** `lib/features/ai/state/embedding_backfill_controller.dart` ‚Äî `_processEntities` has non-trivial arithmetic: `progress = processed / total`, counts for `embedded`, `failed`, `processed`. A Glados property over randomly generated sequences of `didEmbed: bool` and `didThrow: bool` per entity could verify that `processed == embedded + failed + (skipped by cancel)`, `progress == processed / total`, and `embedded + failed <= processed` hold for any input sequence. This is pure math over counters, ideal for property testing. **RESOLVED:** done ‚Äî a property generates per-entity outcome sequences (embed / missing-entity skip / lookup throw) and asserts processed == total, embedded/failed match the outcome counts, embedded+failed <= processed, and progress lands at 1.0.

- [x] **[LOW]** `lib/features/ai/state/consts.dart` ‚Äî `SkillTypeToResponseType.toResponseType` already has a Glados test in `consts_test.dart` ‚Äî well covered. No gap here.
  **RESOLVED:** (confirmed true, no change) `consts_test.dart` lines 147‚Äì157 already drive `toResponseType` through a `glados.Glados(glados.any.skillType, numRuns: 80)` property checked against an independent oracle (`_expectedResponseTypeForSkill`), tagged `glados`. No gap.

- [x] **[LOW]** `lib/features/ai/state/consts.dart` ‚Äî `isLegacyType` could have a Glados property: "for any `AiResponseType` value, `isLegacyType` equals `this == taskSummary || this == checklistUpdates || this == imageGeneration`". However this is trivially verified by the existing static tests; only worth adding Glados if a regression in the formula becomes a concern.
  **RESOLVED:** (confirmed true, no change) the `AiResponseType` enum has only 7 values and the static test `isLegacyType gates exactly the prompt-superseded types` (consts_test.dart lines 130‚Äì144) already asserts the predicate for every value exhaustively. A Glados property over a 7-element enum would re-test the identical finite domain with no added coverage, so it is intentionally omitted per the item's own note.

## Coverage / missing-behavior gaps

- [x] **[HIGH]** `lib/features/ai/state/consts.dart` line 112‚Äì117: `isLegacyType` includes `AiResponseType.imageGeneration`, but `imageGeneration` is NOT annotated `@Deprecated` in the enum (lines 60‚Äì62). It is a current, active skill type. This is likely a logic bug ‚Äî `imageGeneration` should not be classified as a legacy type. No test covers `AiResponseType.imageGeneration.isLegacyType`; the existing `isPromptGenerationType` tests do not touch `isLegacyType`. A test asserting `AiResponseType.imageGeneration.isLegacyType == false` would surface this discrepancy. **RESOLVED (assessed: intentional, not a bug):** `imageGeneration` was deliberately added to `isLegacyType` by the cover-art skill PR (5aa6e65b7) ‚Äî prompt-driven image generation is superseded by `triggerSkillProvider`, while the enum value stays current for skill responses (hence no `@Deprecated`). Documented in `consts.dart` and pinned by a new `isLegacyType` test covering every enum value.

- [x] **[HIGH]** `lib/features/ai/state/unified_ai_controller.dart` ‚Äî `_performInference` error path, lines 329‚Äì344: the second `ref.read(aiConfigByIdProvider(promptId).future)` inside the catch block (to set error status) has its own try/catch that silently swallows failures. There is no test for the case where `promptId` is invalid/not found (config returns `null` or throws) specifically during the error handler. The existing error test (line 507) uses a valid promptId. A test where `promptId` resolves to `null` would exercise line 246's null branch and verify the `Exception('Invalid prompt configuration')` is correctly thrown and caught.

- [x] **[HIGH]** `lib/features/ai/state/unified_ai_controller.dart` ‚Äî `triggerSkillProvider` error handler (lines 596‚Äì603): the entire provider body is wrapped in try/catch that routes to `loggingService.error`. No test exercises a `StateError` being thrown from inside `runner.runTranscription/runImageAnalysis/runPromptGeneration`. The only error test is for `imageGeneration` with null `linkedTaskId` (line 2359). Add coverage for runner methods throwing during execution.

- [x] **[MED]** `lib/features/ai/state/embedding_backfill_controller.dart` ‚Äî `_EmbedResult` class (lines 353‚Äì361) is constructed inside `_processEntities` but its return value is never used by either `backfillCategories` or `backfillAgentReports`. This is dead code. The class and its construction should be removed, or the backfill methods should be updated to use the returned counts (which would enable `backfillCategories` to accumulate `failed` counts from multiple categories correctly ‚Äî currently each category resets the offset only if processed via the offsets, but the returned result is discarded). **RESOLVED:** dead code removed ‚Äî `_processEntities` now returns `Future<void>` and `_EmbedResult` is gone; the lone caller aggregates IDs before the single call, so no accumulation is needed.

- [x] **[MED]** `lib/features/ai/state/embedding_backfill_controller.dart` ‚Äî `backfillCategories` (line 247): the `processedOffset`, `embeddedOffset`, `failedOffset` parameters of `_processEntities` are always passed as 0 even when iterating multiple categories. This means if 2 categories are processed, the second category's `_processEntities` call starts with `processed=0`, overwriting the state from the first call. The test at line 287 (`processes entities across multiple categories`) passes because it uses a single call that aggregates all IDs before passing them to `_processEntities` ‚Äî the multi-category test actually deduplicates into one list so only one `_processEntities` call is made. The offset parameters of `_processEntities` are thus unused dead parameters. No test exercises them being non-zero. **RESOLVED:** removed along with `totalOverride` ‚Äî the counters always start at zero for the single aggregated call.

- [x] **[MED]** `lib/features/ai/state/settings/inference_model_form_controller.dart` line 193: `updatedAt: DateTime.now()` ‚Äî non-deterministic. There is no test for the timestamp set on update; if a test ever needs to assert the updatedAt value it will be flaky. The controller should accept a clock dependency (or a `DateTime Function()` parameter defaulting to `DateTime.now`) for testability. **RESOLVED:** done ‚Äî `updateConfig` now stamps via `clock.now()` (package:clock), and the update test pins `withClock(Clock.fixed(...))` asserting the exact `updatedAt` plus preserved id/createdAt.

- [x] **[MED]** `lib/features/ai/state/profile_automation_providers.dart` ‚Äî `categoryProfileLookup` (lines 32‚Äì37): the closure calls `journalDb.getCategoryById(categoryId)` and reads `category?.defaultProfileId`. No test currently exercises the path where `getCategoryById` returns a non-null category with a `defaultProfileId`. The `profile_automation_providers_test.dart` tests for task profile lookups but the category lookup path is only indirectly covered via `triggerSkillProvider` tests with a real category ID. **RESOLVED:** (stale) the `categoryProfileLookup via resolveForCategory` group covers both the non-null `defaultProfileId` forwarding and the null fallback.

- [x] **[LOW]** `lib/features/ai/state/ai_config_initialization.dart` ‚Äî The `ModelPrepopulationService.backfillNewModels()` error path logs and continues; `ai_config_initialization_test.dart` line 122 covers this. Clean.
  **RESOLVED:** (confirmed true, no change) the test `completes normally and still seeds profiles when model backfill throws` (ai_config_initialization_test.dart lines 122‚Äì152) stubs `getConfigsByType(inferenceProvider)` to throw, then asserts the provider future `completes`, all default profiles are still seeded, and the upgrade pass still runs ‚Äî exactly the swallow-and-continue contract of the backfill try/catch. Clean.

- [x] **[LOW]** `lib/features/ai/state/inference_status_controller.dart` ‚Äî `InferenceRunningController` returns `true` if any of the supplied `responseTypes` is `running`. The existing Glados test covers the `setStatus` ‚Üí read path but there is no test for `InferenceRunningController` with an empty `responseTypes` set (should always return `false`) or with a set that includes a non-running type alongside a running type. These edge cases are not exercised.
  **RESOLVED:** (stale) both edge cases are now exercised in `inference_status_controller_test.dart`. The empty-set case has a dedicated example test `works with empty response types set` (lines 516‚Äì527) asserting `false`. The mixed running/non-running case is covered by `returns true when any inference is running` (lines 341‚Äì413), which sets one type of a two-type set to `running` and asserts `true` while the other stays idle. The Glados property `matches generated multi-response running semantics` (lines 529‚Äì599) additionally generates the empty `responseSetKind` and arbitrary single-running-among-many subsets, checking each against an `any(status == running)` oracle.

---

## Test execution speed opportunities

- [x] **[HIGH]** `test/features/ai/state/unified_ai_controller_test.dart` ‚Äî Lines 341‚Äì362: the mock answer for `runInference` contains two `await Future<void>.delayed(const Duration(milliseconds: 10))` calls (lines 354, 356), and similarly in the linked-entity test at lines 823 and 921. These are real async delays embedded inside `fakeAsync`-driven tests, which defeats the purpose of `fakeAsync` and introduces real wall-clock time into the test run. All occurrences should be removed: the mock callbacks should fire synchronously, and the `async.elapse(const Duration(milliseconds: 10))` calls (lines 387‚Äì389) can remain to advance fake time if the production code uses timers, but the mock itself must not block on real time. **Impact: ~4 tests √ó 20ms real delay = ~80ms saved per run, plus removal of potential flakiness.**

- [x] **[HIGH]** `test/features/ai/state/embedding_backfill_controller_test.dart` line 697: `await Future<void>.delayed(Duration.zero)` used to let `isRunning` propagate. Replace with `await Future<void>.value()` which also yields the event loop but is semantically clearer, or restructure the test to observe state via a listener rather than relying on a scheduler yield. **Impact: minor per-run savings but eliminates a policy violation.**

- [x] **[MED]** `test/features/ai/state/unified_ai_controller_test.dart` ‚Äî Each of the ~56 tests that requires a `ProviderContainer` with overrides constructs its container fresh. The outer `container` variable is replaced mid-test in several tests (e.g., line 526), leaving the original container to be disposed in tearDown. This per-test container creation is correct but the pattern of re-assigning the shared `container` variable mid-test (rather than using `testContainer` + `containersToDispose`) is inconsistent and error-prone. Standardizing on the `testContainer + containersToDispose` pattern throughout eliminates teardown uncertainty. **RESOLVED:** done ‚Äî the two mid-test `container =` reassignments (error-handling and missing-prompt tests) now use local `testContainer` + `containersToDispose`, so the setUp container is never orphaned.

- [x] **[MED]** `test/features/ai/state/active_inference_controller_test.dart` ‚Äî Glados uses `numRuns: 180` with a list length range 0‚Äì55 (line 251). Each run generates up to 55 operations over 2 entity slots √ó 5 response types = 10 independent provider keys. This is CPU-intensive. Reducing to `numRuns: 120` and max list length 40 would still be thorough and cut ~33% of CI Glados time for this test. **RESOLVED:** numRuns reduced to 120 (the operation-list length is sized by glados internally; no explicit 55 cap existed to lower).

- [x] **[MED]** `test/features/ai/state/inference_status_controller_test.dart` ‚Äî Glados uses `numRuns: 180` with list length 0‚Äì55 (inferred from similar pattern). Same recommendation: 120 runs / max 40 operations. **RESOLVED:** numRuns reduced to 120.

- [x] **[LOW]** `test/features/ai/state/unified_ai_controller_test.dart` ‚Äî `setUpAll` registers fallback values that include constructing real `JournalEntry` and `ProviderContainer` instances. These are cheap but if the test file is split as recommended, each split file re-runs `setUpAll`. Centralizing fallback registration in `test/helpers/fallbacks.dart` (which is the prescribed pattern via `registerAllFallbackValues()`) and calling that from `setUpAll` would keep consistency.
  **RESOLVED:** done ‚Äî `setUpAll` now calls `registerAllFallbackValues()` from `test/helpers/fallbacks.dart` (which already provides `FakeAiConfigPrompt`, `fallbackJournalEntity`, `AutomationResult.notHandled`, and `StackTrace.empty`), eliminating the hand-built `JournalEntry`/`Metadata`, `AutomationResult`, and `FakeAiConfigPrompt` fallbacks. Only the two fallbacks the central registry does not yet carry ‚Äî `InferenceStatus.idle` and `<String, dynamic>{}` ‚Äî remain registered locally. The now-unused `journal_entities.dart` and `profile_automation_service.dart` imports were removed. The central registry file itself was not edited (forbidden shared file), so `InferenceStatus`/`Map` stay local.

---

## Summary

- 1 oversized impl file (`unified_ai_controller.dart`, 635 lines ‚Äî 3 provider families in one file)
- 3 oversized test files (`unified_ai_controller_test.dart` 2470, `embedding_backfill_controller_test.dart` 1268, `inference_provider_form_controller_test.dart` 1303) plus 2 test-splitting violations (`*_dirty_state_test.dart`)
- Weak tests: 3 constructor smoke-tests in `profile_automation_providers_test.dart`; inline mock duplication across 2 files; 18 manual GetIt registrations violating `setUpTestGetIt()` rule; repeated `AiConfigPrompt` construction (~8 copies)
- 2 genuine Glados candidates: `availableSkillsForEntityProvider` pure filter logic; `_processEntities` counter arithmetic
- 3 test-speed wins: real `Future.delayed` delays inside `fakeAsync` tests (~80ms/run); `Duration.zero` yield violation; oversized Glados `numRuns` (180‚Üí120) in 2 files
- Biggest single opportunity: split `unified_ai_controller_test.dart` (2470 lines) into ~4 focused files while extracting the shared mock and `AiConfigPrompt` factory ‚Äî this alone would halve average test-file review cost and remove the two most significant quality violations (inline mock + GetIt boilerplate) in the process
